pattern: Filling the sequence left to right and, at every still-empty slot, trying the largest unused value first (placing 1 alone, or placing v at the current slot and again exactly v steps later) greedily builds the lexicographically largest valid arrangement, backtracking only when a choice can't be completed.
hints: Always work on the leftmost position that is still empty, and try to fill it with the biggest value you haven't used yet.
Placing value 1 only ever takes the current slot; placing any value v > 1 takes the current slot and the slot exactly v positions later, so both of those must still be empty.

If a choice leads to a dead end further along, undo it and try the next smaller unused value at that same slot -- because you always try large values first, the first complete fill you find is already the lexicographically largest one.

stress.description: every value across the small legal range
stress.note: domain is only 1..20, so this simply cycles the whole range under the harness's repeated sampling
